findings.evidence: `rg` shows only `abbrev ofHom` at `ComoduleCat.lean:96` and no round-trip lemmas; Mathlib's comparable `ModuleCat` exposes `hom_ofHom`, `ofHom_hom`, `ofHom_id`, `ofHom_comp`, and `ofHom_apply` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Basic.lean:169-186`.
findings.fix: Add the standard `[simp]` round-trip lemmas for `hom`/`ofHom` plus identity, composition, and apply restatements, matching the bundled-category API pattern.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat.ofHom` has no public elimination/round-trip API: there are no `hom_ofHom`, `ofHom_hom`, `ofHom_id`, `ofHom_comp`, or `ofHom_apply` lemmas, so downstream code must rely on reducibility/rfl to move between categorical morphisms and `Comodule.Hom`.

summary: The bundled category exposes `ofHom`/`hom` but not the characteristic round-trip and composition API needed to use them without unfolding. Add the standard simp/restatement lemmas before exposing this as the public comodule-category interface.
